name: TCP SYN Flood
description: High-rate TCP SYN packets from single source — exhausts server half-open connection table. Maps to DoS Hulk/Slowloris in CIC-IDS-2017.

name: UDP Flood
description: High-rate UDP packets from single source — overwhelms network bandwidth and UDP listener queues. Maps to UDP Flood in CIC-IDS-2017.

name: ICMP Flood
description: High-rate ICMP echo request packets — ping flood consuming bandwidth. Maps to ICMP Flood in CIC-IDS-2017.

name: TCP ACK Flood
description: High-rate TCP ACK packets — stateless DoS that bypasses SYN cookies. Maps to DoS Slowhttptest in CIC-IDS-2017.

name: TCP RST Flood
description: High-rate TCP RST packets — terminates legitimate connections. Maps to DoS GoldenEye in CIC-IDS-2017.

name: TCP SYN Port Scan
description: SYN packets to many different ports in short time — classic TCP SYN scan (nmap -sS). Maps to PortScan in CIC-IDS-2017.

name: TCP FIN Scan
description: FIN packets to many ports — stealthy FIN scan bypasses basic firewalls. Open ports drop FIN, closed ports reply RST.

name: TCP NULL Scan
description: TCP packets with NO flags set — NULL scan probes for open ports on non-Windows systems. Closed ports reply RST, open ports drop silently.

name: TCP XMAS Scan
description: TCP packets with FIN+PSH+URG flags — XMAS scan lights all flags like a Christmas tree. Evasion technique against stateless ACL firewalls.

name: UDP Port Scan
description: UDP packets to many different ports — UDP scan discovers open UDP services. Slower than TCP scans; closed ports reply ICMP Port Unreachable.

name: Heartbleed Attack
description: High-rate connections to HTTPS/TLS port 443 — detects Heartbleed exploitation attempt (CVE-2014-0160) and HTTPS brute force.

name: FTP Brute Force
description: Repeated TCP connections to FTP port 21 — credential brute force against FTP service. Maps to FTP-Patator in CIC-IDS-2017.

name: SSH Brute Force
description: Repeated TCP connections to SSH port 22 — automated password guessing. Maps to SSH-Patator in CIC-IDS-2017.

name: Telnet Brute Force
description: Repeated TCP connections to Telnet port 23 — brute force against legacy unencrypted remote access. Telnet transmits credentials in plaintext.

name: RDP Brute Force
description: Repeated TCP connections to RDP port 3389 — automated credential stuffing against Windows Remote Desktop. High-value target for ransomware groups.

name: HTTP Flood
description: High-rate TCP connections to HTTP port 80 — Layer 7 flood overwhelming the web server connection queue. Equivalent to HTTP GET flood.

name: HTTPS Flood
description: High-rate TCP connections to HTTPS port 443 — SSL/TLS handshake flood; each connection forces expensive crypto operations on server.

name: SMTP Flood
description: High-rate connections to SMTP port 25 — email server DoS or spam relay attempt. Can exhaust mail queue resources.

name: DNS Amplification
description: High-rate UDP to DNS port 53 — DNS amplification/reflection attack using open resolvers. Small queries return large responses amplifying traffic 50-70x.

name: MySQL Brute Force
description: Repeated TCP connections to MySQL port 3306 — automated credential brute force against MySQL database server.

name: SMB Flood
description: High-rate connections to SMB port 445 — EternalBlue/WannaCry/NotPetya exploit vector. SMB on Windows is a critical attack surface.

name: NetBIOS Flood
description: High-rate UDP to NetBIOS Name Service port 137 — NetBIOS amplification or enumeration flood on Windows networks.

name: LDAP Flood
description: High-rate connections to LDAP port 389 — LDAP amplification attack or directory service brute force. LDAP amplification factor can reach 46-70x.
reference: CVE-2021-44228 Log4Shell used LDAP; US-CERT AA21-209A

name: MSSQL Brute Force
description: Repeated TCP connections to MSSQL port 1433 — automated credential attack against Microsoft SQL Server. Common ransomware pivot point.

name: PostgreSQL Brute Force
description: Repeated TCP connections to PostgreSQL port 5432 — automated credential brute force against PostgreSQL database.

name: TCP SYN-ACK Flood
description: High-rate TCP SYN-ACK packets — reflection/amplification DoS using spoofed SYN to third-party servers; their SYN-ACK responses flood the victim.

name: TCP FIN Flood
description: High-rate TCP FIN packets — closes connections prematurely, disrupts established sessions and overwhelms TCP state tables.

name: TCP PSH-ACK Flood
description: High-rate TCP PSH+ACK packets — application-layer data flood forcing the receiving end to process each packet immediately, exhausting CPU.

name: WinRM Brute Force
description: Repeated connections to Windows Remote Management port 5985 — credential brute force for lateral movement via PowerShell remoting.

name: Elasticsearch Attack
description: High-rate connections to Elasticsearch REST API port 9200 — data exfiltration, index deletion, or exploitation of unauthenticated clusters.
